question_text: Study the diagram showing the Marketing Mix (4 Ps). According to the diagram, which element deals with advertising, personal selling, and sales promotion?
image_alt: A hub-and-spoke diagram with 'MARKETING MIX' at the centre. Four spokes lead to: (1) Product — branding, packaging, labelling, (2) Price — pricing strategies, factors affecting price, (3) Place — channels of distribution, physical distribution, (4) Promotion — advertising, personal selling, sales promotion, publicity. Each element is colour-coded with examples listed beneath.
explanation: The diagram clearly shows four elements of the Marketing Mix. Promotion is the element that deals with advertising, personal selling, sales promotion, and publicity. It covers all communication tools used to inform and persuade customers about the product.
subject: cuet-business-studies

elimination_hints.hint: Product deals with branding, packaging, and labelling — not advertising or sales promotion.
elimination_hints.misconception: Confusing product decisions with promotional tools

elimination_hints.hint: Price deals with pricing strategies and factors affecting price — not advertising.
Place deals with channels of distribution and physical distribution — not selling techniques.

question_text: Study the flowchart showing Channels of Distribution. According to the diagram, how many intermediaries are involved in 'Channel C' (Manufacturer → Wholesaler → Retailer → Consumer)?
image_alt: A diagram showing 4 channels of distribution in parallel: Channel A (Zero-level) — Manufacturer → Consumer (direct, no intermediary). Channel B (One-level) — Manufacturer → Retailer → Consumer (1 intermediary). Channel C (Two-level) — Manufacturer → Wholesaler → Retailer → Consumer (2 intermediaries). Channel D (Three-level) — Manufacturer → Agent → Wholesaler → Retailer → Consumer (3 intermediaries). Each channel is shown as a horizontal flow with arrows.
explanation: Channel C is the two-level channel: Manufacturer → Wholesaler → Retailer → Consumer. It has 2 intermediaries — the Wholesaler and the Retailer. The manufacturer sells to the wholesaler, who sells to the retailer, who finally sells to the consumer.
subject: cuet-business-studies

elimination_hints.hint: One intermediary is Channel B (one-level). Channel C has both Wholesaler AND Retailer.
elimination_hints.misconception: Counting only the retailer as intermediary

elimination_hints.hint: Three intermediaries is Channel D (three-level) which includes an Agent. Channel C has no agent.
elimination_hints.misconception: Reading the wrong channel from the diagram

elimination_hints.hint: Zero intermediaries is Channel A (direct selling). Channel C clearly shows Wholesaler and Retailer.

question_text: Study the diagram showing Product Labelling information. According to the diagram, which of the following is NOT a mandatory information item shown on the label?
image_alt: An annotated product label (rectangular box) showing different mandatory and optional information items with arrows pointing to each area: Brand Name (top), Net Weight/Quantity, MRP (Maximum Retail Price), Date of Manufacture, Expiry/Best Before Date, Ingredients/Composition, Manufacturer's Name and Address, Batch/Lot Number, FSSAI License Number, Bar Code. One item marked with a star as 'Optional': Customer Testimonial. The label is colour-coded — green for mandatory items and yellow for the optional item.
explanation: The diagram shows mandatory label information (in green) and optional information (in yellow). Customer Testimonial is marked as optional — it is NOT a mandatory requirement. All other items like MRP, manufacturing date, expiry date, ingredients, etc. are mandatory under various consumer protection laws.
subject: cuet-business-studies

elimination_hints.hint: MRP is mandatory under the Legal Metrology Act — it is shown in green (mandatory) on the label.
Date of manufacture is mandatory — it helps consumers know the freshness and age of the product.
Ingredients are mandatory under FSSAI regulations for food products — shown in green on the label.
